audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Peter text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for living stones, royal priesthood, honorable conduct, civic order, enslaved household passage with anti-abuse and anti-slavery safeguards, Messiah's suffering.

verses.restored: Therefore putting away all harmfulness and all deceit and hypocrisies and envies and all slanders,
as newborn infants, long for the unmixed milk of the word, so that by it you may grow into salvation/healing,
if indeed you tasted that the Lord is kind.
Coming to him, a living stone, rejected by humans but chosen and precious before the Divine,
you yourselves also, as living stones, are being built as spiritual house, into holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to the Divine through Jesus Messiah.
Because it stands in Scripture: Look, I place in Zion a stone, chosen, precious cornerstone, and the one trusting upon him will certainly not be put to shame.
Therefore the honor is for you who trust, but for those who do not trust, the stone that the builders rejected, this became head of corner,
and a stone of stumbling and rock of offense; they stumble by disobeying the word, into which also they were appointed.
But you are chosen lineage, royal priesthood, holy nation, people for possession, so that you may proclaim the excellencies of the One calling you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
Once not a people, but now people of Divine; once not having received mercy, but now having received mercy.
Beloved, I appeal to you as sojourners and resident foreigners to abstain from fleshly desires that war against the soul/life,
having your conduct beautiful among the nations, so that in what they speak against you as wrongdoers, from your beautiful works they may see and glorify the Divine in day of visitation.
Be arranged under every human institution because of the Lord, whether to king as superior,
or to governors as sent through him for justice upon wrongdoers and praise of those doing good.
For so is the will of the Divine, doing good to silence the ignorance of foolish humans.
As free ones, and not having freedom as cover for harmfulness, but as servants of Divine.
Honor all. Love the siblinghood. Fear/revere the Divine. Honor the king.
Household servants/enslaved ones, be arranged under masters with all reverence, not only the good and gentle but also the crooked.
For this is grace, if because of consciousness of Divine someone endures griefs while suffering unjustly.
For what credit if, sinning/misaligned and being struck, you endure? But if doing good and suffering you endure, this is grace before the Divine.
For into this you were called, because Messiah also suffered on behalf of you, leaving you an example, so that you might follow his tracks.
He did no sin/misalignment, nor was deceit found in his mouth.
When reviled, he did not revile back; suffering, he did not threaten, but handed himself to the One judging justly.
He himself bore our sins/misalignments in his body upon the tree, so that, having died to sins/misalignments, we might live to righteousness/justice; by his wound you were healed.
For you were like sheep wandering, but now you turned back to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls/lives.

safety_note: 1 Peter 2:18-25 must not be used to sanctify slavery, workplace coercion, domestic abuse, or passive endurance of harm. The text addresses ancient household servants/enslaved people under unjust conditions and points to Messiah's non-retaliatory trust in Divine justice, not to enabling abusers. Reception-history audit required.
translation_notes: Living stones/royal priesthood language draws Israel Scripture imagery into a Messiah community and should not erase Jewish Israel.
Submission language is bounded by doing good, freedom, Divine service, and justice.
